properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 3:00PM MDT until July 22 at 4:15PM MDT by NWS Pueblo CO
properties.description:
At 300 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing heavy
rain across the warned area. Between 0.5 and 1 inch of rain has
fallen. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms.

SOURCE...Radar indicated.

IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, urban areas,
highways, streets and underpasses as well as other poor
drainage and low-lying areas.

Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Mainly rural areas of Central Chaffee County.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 2:52PM MDT until July 22 at 4:30PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Small stream flooding caused by excessive rainfall
continues.

* WHERE...South Central Larimer County in north central Colorado...

* WHEN...Until 430 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Rises in small streams and normally dry creeks.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

- At 251 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated heavy rain continuing
due to thunderstorms. This may cause small stream flooding.
Between 1 and 1.5 inches of rain have fallen.

- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Pingree Park.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 2:36PM MDT until July 22 at 5:30PM MDT by NWS Denver CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by excessive rainfall is expected.

* WHERE...West Central Jefferson County in central Colorado...
Northeastern Park County in central Colorado...

* WHEN...Until 530 PM MDT.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...

- At 236 PM MDT, Doppler radar and automated rain gauges
indicated heavy rain due to thunderstorms. Minor flooding is
ongoing or expected to begin shortly in the advisory area.
Between 0.5 and 1.5 inches of rain have fallen.

- Some locations that will experience flooding include...
Pine Junction, Bailey, Pine Grove, Shaffers Crossing and
Glenisle.

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 2:08PM MDT until July 22 at 5:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
East Central Montrose County in west central Colorado...

* Until 500 PM MDT.

* At 208 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across Montrose, stretching east over the South Rim
burn scar. The expected rainfall rate is 2 to 3 inches in 1 hour.
Additional rainfall amounts of 0.5 to 1 inch are possible in the
warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly.

Excessive rainfall over the burn scar will result in debris flow
moving through the South Rim. The debris flow can consist of rock,
mud, vegetation and other loose materials.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Montrose and the South Rim burn scar.

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 1:46PM MDT until July 22 at 4:45PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
North Central Montrose County in west central Colorado...

* Until 445 PM MDT.

* At 146 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 0.7 and 1.2 inches of
rain have fallen. The expected rainfall rate is 1 to 2 inches in 1
hour. Additional rainfall amounts of 0.5 to 1 inch are possible in
the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to begin
shortly.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
mainly rural areas of North Central Montrose County

This includes the following streams and drainages...
Dry Creek, Spring Creek and Coal Creek.

properties.headline: Flash Flood Warning issued July 22 at 12:43PM MDT until July 22 at 3:45PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
FFWGJT

The National Weather Service in Grand Junction has issued a

* Flash Flood Warning for...
East Central San Miguel County in southwestern Colorado...

* Until 345 PM MDT.

* At 1243 PM MDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. The expected rainfall rate is 1
to 2 inches in 1 hour. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected to
begin shortly.

HAZARD...Life threatening flash flooding. Thunderstorms
producing flash flooding.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Life threatening flash flooding of creeks and streams,
urban areas, highways, streets and underpasses.

* Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Sawpit and Fall Creek.

properties.headline: Flood Advisory issued July 22 at 11:04AM MDT until July 22 at 9:00PM MDT by NWS Grand Junction CO
properties.description:
* WHAT...Flash flooding and debris flows are possible, flooding
caused by excessive rainfall over the Gold Mountain Fire is
possible.

* WHERE...Gold Mountain Fire in Ouray County Colorado, including
Dexter and Cutler Creeks and Highway 550.

* WHEN...Until 900 PM MDT Wednesday.

* IMPACTS...Excessive rainfall is possible over the Gold Mountain
Burn Scar which could lead to flash flooding and/or debris flows,
particularly along highway 550 between Cutler Creek and Dexter
Creek. The debris flow can consist of rock, mud, vegetation and
other loose materials.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 1057 AM MDT, The probability of heavy rainfall will
increase after 12 PM this afternoon, lasting through the
evening. If thunderstorms develop over the Gold Mountain burn
scar, debris flows are likely within Cutler and Dexter Creek
drainages, including Highway 550.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood
